title: quercus × alvesii
intro: Quercus × alvesii is a hybrid plant between Quercus robur and Quercus faginea, also known as the pedunculate oak and the Portuguese oak, respectively. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its high tannin content, which has been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Quercus × alvesii include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to protect against heart disease and certain types of cancer. The plant is typically prepared into herbal remedies such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support digestive health and immune function.
